Compared to the enormous deployments of supervised and unsupervised learning models, attempts to use reinforcement learning in medical image analysis are scarce. This review article could serve as the stepping-stone for related research. Significance: From our observation, though reinforcement learning has gradually gained momentum in recent years, many researchers in the medical analysis field find it hard to understand and deploy in clinics. One cause is lacking well-organized review articles targeting readers lacking professional computer science backgrounds. Rather than providing a comprehensive list of all reinforcement learning models in medical image analysis, this paper may help the readers to learn how to formulate and solve their medical image analysis research as reinforcement learning problems. Approach & Results: We selected published articles from Google Scholar and PubMed. Considering the scarcity of related articles, we also included some outstanding newest preprints. The papers are carefully reviewed and categorized according to the type of image analysis task. We first review the basic concepts and popular models of reinforcement learning. Then we explore the applications of reinforcement learning models in landmark detection. Finally, we conclude the article by discussing the reviewed reinforcement learning approaches' limitations and possible improvements.
abstract_zh: 动机：医学图像分析涉及协助医生对病变或解剖结构进行定性和定量分析的任务，显着提高诊断和预后的准确性和可靠性。传统上，这些任务由医生或医学物理学家完成，导致两个主要问题：（i）效率低； (ii) 因个人经验而产生偏见。在过去的十年中，许多机器学习方法已被应用于加速和自动化图像分析过程。与监督和无监督学习模型的大量部署相比，在医学图像分析中使用强化学习的尝试很少。这篇综述文章可以作为相关研究的垫脚石。意义：根据我们的观察，虽然强化学习近年来逐渐获得发展势头，但许多医学分析领域的研究人员发现其难以理解并在临床中部署。原因之一是缺乏针对缺乏专业计算机科学背景的读者的组织良好的评论文章。本文不是提供医学图像分析中所有强化学习模型的全面列表，而是可以帮助读者学习如何将他们的医学图像分析研究制定和解决为强化学习问题。方法和结果：我们选择了来自 Google Scholar 和 PubMed 的已发表文章。考虑到相关文章的稀缺性，我们还收录了一些优秀的最新预印本。这些论文根据图像分析任务的类型进行了仔细审查和分类。我们首先回顾一下强化学习的基本概念和流行模型。然后我们探索强化学习模型在地标检测中的应用。最后，我们通过讨论所审查的强化学习方法的局限性和可能的​​改进来结束本文。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了强化学习在医学图像分析中的概念、应用、挑战及未来方向，旨在为相关研究提供指导。
AI.motivation: 传统医学图像分析依赖人工操作，存在效率低和主观偏差问题。尽管机器学习已广泛应用，但强化学习在该领域的研究仍较少，缺乏针对非计算机背景读者的系统性综述。
AI.method: 通过Google Scholar和PubMed筛选文献，并纳入最新预印本。按图像分析任务类型分类，回顾强化学习基础模型，探讨其在医学图像关键点检测等场景的应用，并分析局限性。
AI.result: 系统梳理了强化学习在医学图像分析中的应用案例，总结了当前方法的不足，如数据稀缺性和算法可解释性问题，并提出了改进方向。
AI.conclusion: 强化学习在医学图像分析中具有潜力，但需解决数据获取、模型泛化及跨学科协作等挑战，未来需更多临床验证与算法优化。

authors_text: Dieqiao Feng,Carla Gomes,Bart Selman
title: Left Heavy Tails and the Effectiveness of the Policy and Value Networks in DNN-based best-first search for Sokoban Planning
title_zh: 基于 DNN 的推箱子规划最佳优先搜索中的左重尾以及政策和价值网络的有效性
abstract: Despite the success of practical solvers in various NP-complete domains such as SAT and CSP as well as using deep reinforcement learning to tackle two-player games such as Go, certain classes of PSPACE-hard planning problems have remained out of reach. Even carefully designed domain-specialized solvers can fail quickly due to the exponential search space on hard instances. Recent works that combine traditional search methods, such as best-first search and Monte Carlo tree search, with Deep Neural Networks' (DNN) heuristics have shown promising progress and can solve a significant number of hard planning instances beyond specialized solvers. To better understand why these approaches work, we studied the interplay of the policy and value networks of DNN-based best-first search on Sokoban and show the surprising effectiveness of the policy network, further enhanced by the value network, as a guiding heuristic for the search. To further understand the phenomena, we studied the cost distribution of the search algorithms and found that Sokoban instances can have heavy-tailed runtime distributions, with tails both on the left and right-hand sides. In particular, for the first time, we show the existence of \textit{left heavy tails} and propose an abstract tree model that can empirically explain the appearance of these tails. The experiments show the critical role of the policy network as a powerful heuristic guiding the search, which can lead to left heavy tails with polynomial scaling by avoiding exploring exponentially sized subtrees. Our results also demonstrate the importance of random restarts, as are widely used in traditional combinatorial solvers, for DNN-based search methods to avoid left and right heavy tails.
summary: Despite the success of practical solvers in various NP-complete domains such as SAT and CSP as well as using deep reinforcement learning to tackle two-player games such as Go, certain classes of PSPACE-hard planning problems have remained out of reach. Even carefully designed domain-specialized solvers can fail quickly due to the exponential search space on hard instances. Recent works that combine traditional search methods, such as best-first search and Monte Carlo tree search, with Deep Neural Networks' (DNN) heuristics have shown promising progress and can solve a significant number of hard planning instances beyond specialized solvers. To better understand why these approaches work, we studied the interplay of the policy and value networks of DNN-based best-first search on Sokoban and show the surprising effectiveness of the policy network, further enhanced by the value network, as a guiding heuristic for the search. To further understand the phenomena, we studied the cost distribution of the search algorithms and found that Sokoban instances can have heavy-tailed runtime distributions, with tails both on the left and right-hand sides. In particular, for the first time, we show the existence of \textit{left heavy tails} and propose an abstract tree model that can empirically explain the appearance of these tails. The experiments show the critical role of the policy network as a powerful heuristic guiding the search, which can lead to left heavy tails with polynomial scaling by avoiding exploring exponentially sized subtrees. Our results also demonstrate the importance of random restarts, as are widely used in traditional combinatorial solvers, for DNN-based search methods to avoid left and right heavy tails.
abstract_zh: 尽管实际求解器在 SAT 和 CSP 等各种 NP 完全领域取得了成功，并且使用深度强化学习来解决围棋等两人游戏，但某些类别的 PSPACE 困难规划问题仍然遥不可及。由于硬实例上的指数搜索空间，即使是精心设计的领域专用求解器也可能很快失败。最近的工作将传统搜索方法（例如最佳优先搜索和蒙特卡罗树搜索）与深度神经网络（DNN）启发式相结合，已经取得了可喜的进展，并且可以解决大量超出专业求解器的硬规划实例。为了更好地理解为什么这些方法有效，我们研究了 Sokoban 上基于 DNN 的最佳优先搜索的策略和价值网络的相互作用，并展示了策略网络令人惊讶的有效性，并通过价值网络进一步增强，作为搜索的指导启发式。为了进一步理解这一现象，我们研究了搜索算法的成本分布，发现推箱子实例可以具有重尾运行时分布，即左右两侧都有尾部。特别是，我们第一次证明了 \textit{left Heavy tails} 的存在，并提出了一个抽象树模型，可以凭经验解释这些尾巴的出现。实验显示了策略网络作为指导搜索的强大启发式的关键作用，它可以通过避免探索指数大小的子树来导致具有多项式缩放的左重尾。我们的结果还证明了随机重启的重要性，随机重启广泛应用于传统组合求解器中，对于基于 DNN 的搜索方法以避免左右重尾。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了基于深度神经网络（DNN）的最优优先搜索在Sokoban规划中的策略网络和价值网络的交互作用，并揭示了左重尾现象对搜索效率的影响。
AI.motivation: 尽管现有求解器在NP完全问题和围棋等博弈中表现优异，但PSPACE难的Sokoban规划仍因指数级搜索空间难以解决，需探索DNN与传统搜索结合的新方法。
AI.method: 将最佳优先搜索与DNN启发式结合，通过分析搜索成本分布，提出抽象树模型解释左重尾现象，并验证策略网络与价值网络的协同作用。
AI.result: 策略网络作为高效启发式能避免指数级子树探索，产生左重尾分布；随机重启对避免左右重尾均至关重要，实验表明DNN搜索方法显著超越传统求解器。
AI.conclusion: 策略网络是引导搜索的核心，随机重启机制不可或缺，左重尾模型为理解DNN搜索行为提供了理论依据。

search_text: Left Heavy Tails and the Effectiveness of the Policy and Value Networks in DNN-based best-first search for Sokoban Planning 基于 DNN 的推箱子规划最佳优先搜索中的左重尾以及政策和价值网络的有效性 Despite the success of practical solvers in various NP-complete domains such as SAT and CSP as well as using deep reinforcement learning to tackle two-player games such as Go, certain classes of PSPACE-hard planning problems have remained out of reach. Even carefully designed domain-specialized solvers can fail quickly due to the exponential search space on hard instances. Recent works that combine traditional search methods, such as best-first search and Monte Carlo tree search, with Deep Neural Networks' (DNN) heuristics have shown promising progress and can solve a significant number of hard planning instances beyond specialized solvers. To better understand why these approaches work, we studied the interplay of the policy and value networks of DNN-based best-first search on Sokoban and show the surprising effectiveness of the policy network, further enhanced by the value network, as a guiding heuristic for the search. To further understand the phenomena, we studied the cost distribution of the search algorithms and found that Sokoban instances can have heavy-tailed runtime distributions, with tails both on the left and right-hand sides. In particular, for the first time, we show the existence of \textit{left heavy tails} and propose an abstract tree model that can empirically explain the appearance of these tails. The experiments show the critical role of the policy network as a powerful heuristic guiding the search, which can lead to left heavy tails with polynomial scaling by avoiding exploring exponentially sized subtrees. Our results also demonstrate the importance of random restarts, as are widely used in traditional combinatorial solvers, for DNN-based search methods to avoid left and right heavy tails. 尽管实际求解器在 SAT 和 CSP 等各种 NP 完全领域取得了成功，并且使用深度强化学习来解决围棋等两人游戏，但某些类别的 PSPACE 困难规划问题仍然遥不可及。由于硬实例上的指数搜索空间，即使是精心设计的领域专用求解器也可能很快失败。最近的工作将传统搜索方法（例如最佳优先搜索和蒙特卡罗树搜索）与深度神经网络（DNN）启发式相结合，已经取得了可喜的进展，并且可以解决大量超出专业求解器的硬规划实例。为了更好地理解为什么这些方法有效，我们研究了 Sokoban 上基于 DNN 的最佳优先搜索的策略和价值网络的相互作用，并展示了策略网络令人惊讶的有效性，并通过价值网络进一步增强，作为搜索的指导启发式。为了进一步理解这一现象，我们研究了搜索算法的成本分布，发现推箱子实例可以具有重尾运行时分布，即左右两侧都有尾部。特别是，我们第一次证明了 \textit{left Heavy tails} 的存在，并提出了一个抽象树模型，可以凭经验解释这些尾巴的出现。实验显示了策略网络作为指导搜索的强大启发式的关键作用，它可以通过避免探索指数大小的子树来导致具有多项式缩放的左重尾。我们的结果还证明了随机重启的重要性，随机重启广泛应用于传统组合求解器中，对于基于 DNN 的搜索方法以避免左右重尾。 本文研究了基于深度神经网络（DNN）的最优优先搜索在Sokoban规划中的策略网络和价值网络的交互作用，并揭示了左重尾现象对搜索效率的影响。 Dieqiao Feng,Carla Gomes,Bart Selman cs.AI

authors_text: Matthew L. Elwin,Billie Strong,Randy A. Freeman,Kevin M. Lynch
title: Human-Multirobot Collaborative Mobile Manipulation: the Omnid Mocobots
title_zh: 人机多机器人协作移动操控：Omnid Mocobots
comment: 8 pages, 10 figures. Videos available at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SEuFfONryL0. Submitted to IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters (RA-L)
comments: 8 pages, 10 figures. Videos available at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SEuFfONryL0. Submitted to IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters (RA-L)
abstract: The Omnid human-collaborative mobile manipulators are an experimental platform for testing control architectures for autonomous and human-collaborative multirobot mobile manipulation. An Omnid consists of a mecanum-wheel omnidirectional mobile base and a series-elastic Delta-type parallel manipulator, and it is a specific implementation of a broader class of mobile collaborative robots ("mocobots") suitable for safe human co-manipulation of delicate, flexible, and articulated payloads. Key features of mocobots include passive compliance, for the safety of the human and the payload, and high-fidelity end-effector force control independent of the potentially imprecise motions of the mobile base. We describe general considerations for the design of teams of mocobots; the design of the Omnids in light of these considerations; manipulator and mobile base controllers to achieve useful multirobot collaborative behaviors; and initial experiments in human-multirobot collaborative mobile manipulation of large, unwieldy payloads. For these experiments, the only communication among the humans and Omnids is mechanical, through the payload.
summary: The Omnid human-collaborative mobile manipulators are an experimental platform for testing control architectures for autonomous and human-collaborative multirobot mobile manipulation. An Omnid consists of a mecanum-wheel omnidirectional mobile base and a series-elastic Delta-type parallel manipulator, and it is a specific implementation of a broader class of mobile collaborative robots ("mocobots") suitable for safe human co-manipulation of delicate, flexible, and articulated payloads. Key features of mocobots include passive compliance, for the safety of the human and the payload, and high-fidelity end-effector force control independent of the potentially imprecise motions of the mobile base. We describe general considerations for the design of teams of mocobots; the design of the Omnids in light of these considerations; manipulator and mobile base controllers to achieve useful multirobot collaborative behaviors; and initial experiments in human-multirobot collaborative mobile manipulation of large, unwieldy payloads. For these experiments, the only communication among the humans and Omnids is mechanical, through the payload.
abstract_zh: Omnid 人机协作移动操纵器是一个实验平台，用于测试自主和人机协作多机器人移动操纵的控制架构。 Omnid 由麦克纳姆轮全向移动底座和串联弹性 Delta 型并联机械手组成，是更广泛的移动协作机器人（“mocobots”）类别的具体实现，适用于人类安全地共同操纵精致、灵活和铰接式有效负载。 Mocobot 的主要特点包括被动合规性（以确保人类和有效负载的安全），以及独立于移动基座潜在不精确运动的高保真末端执行器力控制。我们描述了设计移动机器人团队的一般考虑因素； Omnid 的设计就是考虑到这些因素；机械手和移动底座控制器，以实现有用的多机器人协作行为；以及人类多机器人协作移动操纵大型笨重有效载荷的初步实验。对于这些实验，人类和 Omnid 之间唯一的通信是通过有效负载进行机械通信。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一种名为Omnid的人机协作移动机械臂平台，用于测试自主和人机协作的多机器人移动操作控制架构。
AI.motivation: 为实现安全的人机协作操作，需开发具有被动顺应性、高精度末端力控制能力的移动协作机器人（mocobots），以处理精密、柔性或复杂负载。
AI.method: 设计包含全向移动底盘和系列弹性Delta并联机械臂的Omnid系统，通过被动顺应性保障安全，并开发独立于移动底盘运动的高精度力控算法，利用机械负载实现人机协作通信。
AI.result: 成功实现了多人与Omnid机器人协作操作大型负载的实验，验证了无需通信仅通过负载传递的机械协作方式的有效性。
AI.conclusion: Omnid mocobots通过被动顺应性和力控技术，展示了人机协作移动操作的安全性与可行性，为复杂负载协作提供了新思路。

authors_text: Ian D. Miller,Fernando Cladera,Trey Smith,Camillo Jose Taylor,Vijay Kumar
title: Stronger Together: Air-Ground Robotic Collaboration Using Semantics
title_zh: 强强联手：使用语义的空地机器人协作
abstract: In this work, we present an end-to-end heterogeneous multi-robot system framework where ground robots are able to localize, plan, and navigate in a semantic map created in real time by a high-altitude quadrotor. The ground robots choose and deconflict their targets independently, without any external intervention. Moreover, they perform cross-view localization by matching their local maps with the overhead map using semantics. The communication backbone is opportunistic and distributed, allowing the entire system to operate with no external infrastructure aside from GPS for the quadrotor. We extensively tested our system by performing different missions on top of our framework over multiple experiments in different environments. Our ground robots travelled over 6 km autonomously with minimal intervention in the real world and over 96 km in simulation without interventions.
summary: In this work, we present an end-to-end heterogeneous multi-robot system framework where ground robots are able to localize, plan, and navigate in a semantic map created in real time by a high-altitude quadrotor. The ground robots choose and deconflict their targets independently, without any external intervention. Moreover, they perform cross-view localization by matching their local maps with the overhead map using semantics. The communication backbone is opportunistic and distributed, allowing the entire system to operate with no external infrastructure aside from GPS for the quadrotor. We extensively tested our system by performing different missions on top of our framework over multiple experiments in different environments. Our ground robots travelled over 6 km autonomously with minimal intervention in the real world and over 96 km in simulation without interventions.
abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们提出了一个端到端异构多机器人系统框架，其中地面机器人能够在由高空四旋翼飞行器实时创建的语义地图中进行定位、规划和导航。地面机器人独立选择目标并消除冲突，无需任何外部干预。此外，他们通过使用语义将本地地图与高架地图进行匹配来执行跨视图定位。通信骨干是机会性和分布式的，使得整个系统除了四旋翼飞行器的 GPS 之外无需外部基础设施即可运行。我们通过在不同环境中进行多次实验，在我们的框架之上执行不同的任务，对我们的系统进行了广泛的测试。我们的地面机器人在现实世界中以最少的干预自主行驶了 6 公里以上，在没有干预的模拟中行驶了 96 公里以上。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种端到端的异构多机器人系统框架，地面机器人通过高空四旋翼无人机实时生成的语义地图进行定位、规划和导航，并实现自主目标选择与冲突解决。
AI.motivation: 现有机器人系统常依赖外部基础设施或人工干预，难以实现复杂环境下的自主协作。本文旨在构建无需外部支持的自主化空地协同机器人系统。
AI.method: 通过四旋翼无人机实时构建语义地图，地面机器人基于语义信息进行跨视角定位与路径规划，采用分布式机会通信机制，无需额外基础设施。
AI.result: 实际环境中地面机器人自主行驶超6公里，仿真环境中达到96公里，全程干预极少，验证了系统的有效性与鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 该框架展示了基于语义的空地机器人协作潜力，为复杂场景下的自主化多机器人系统提供了可行方案。

method: 通过四旋翼无人机实时构建语义地图，地面机器人基于语义信息进行跨视角定位与路径规划，采用分布式机会通信机制，无需额外基础设施。
result: 实际环境中地面机器人自主行驶超6公里，仿真环境中达到96公里，全程干预极少，验证了系统的有效性与鲁棒性。
conclusion: 该框架展示了基于语义的空地机器人协作潜力，为复杂场景下的自主化多机器人系统提供了可行方案。
search_text: Stronger Together: Air-Ground Robotic Collaboration Using Semantics 强强联手：使用语义的空地机器人协作 In this work, we present an end-to-end heterogeneous multi-robot system framework where ground robots are able to localize, plan, and navigate in a semantic map created in real time by a high-altitude quadrotor. The ground robots choose and deconflict their targets independently, without any external intervention. Moreover, they perform cross-view localization by matching their local maps with the overhead map using semantics. The communication backbone is opportunistic and distributed, allowing the entire system to operate with no external infrastructure aside from GPS for the quadrotor. We extensively tested our system by performing different missions on top of our framework over multiple experiments in different environments. Our ground robots travelled over 6 km autonomously with minimal intervention in the real world and over 96 km in simulation without interventions. 在这项工作中，我们提出了一个端到端异构多机器人系统框架，其中地面机器人能够在由高空四旋翼飞行器实时创建的语义地图中进行定位、规划和导航。地面机器人独立选择目标并消除冲突，无需任何外部干预。此外，他们通过使用语义将本地地图与高架地图进行匹配来执行跨视图定位。通信骨干是机会性和分布式的，使得整个系统除了四旋翼飞行器的 GPS 之外无需外部基础设施即可运行。我们通过在不同环境中进行多次实验，在我们的框架之上执行不同的任务，对我们的系统进行了广泛的测试。我们的地面机器人在现实世界中以最少的干预自主行驶了 6 公里以上，在没有干预的模拟中行驶了 96 公里以上。 本文提出了一种端到端的异构多机器人系统框架，地面机器人通过高空四旋翼无人机实时生成的语义地图进行定位、规划和导航，并实现自主目标选择与冲突解决。 Ian D. Miller,Fernando Cladera,Trey Smith,Camillo Jose Taylor,Vijay Kumar cs.RO

authors_text: Damian Y. Romero Diaz,Magdalena Anioł,John Culnan
title: Collecting high-quality adversarial data for machine reading comprehension tasks with humans and models in the loop
title_zh: 为人类和模型循环的机器阅读理解任务收集高质量的对抗数据
comment: 8 pages, 3 figures, for more information about the shared task please go to https://dadcworkshop.github.io/
comments: 8 pages, 3 figures, for more information about the shared task please go to https://dadcworkshop.github.io/
abstract: We present our experience as annotators in the creation of high-quality, adversarial machine-reading-comprehension data for extractive QA for Task 1 of the First Workshop on Dynamic Adversarial Data Collection (DADC). DADC is an emergent data collection paradigm with both models and humans in the loop. We set up a quasi-experimental annotation design and perform quantitative analyses across groups with different numbers of annotators focusing on successful adversarial attacks, cost analysis, and annotator confidence correlation. We further perform a qualitative analysis of our perceived difficulty of the task given the different topics of the passages in our dataset and conclude with recommendations and suggestions that might be of value to people working on future DADC tasks and related annotation interfaces.
summary: We present our experience as annotators in the creation of high-quality, adversarial machine-reading-comprehension data for extractive QA for Task 1 of the First Workshop on Dynamic Adversarial Data Collection (DADC). DADC is an emergent data collection paradigm with both models and humans in the loop. We set up a quasi-experimental annotation design and perform quantitative analyses across groups with different numbers of annotators focusing on successful adversarial attacks, cost analysis, and annotator confidence correlation. We further perform a qualitative analysis of our perceived difficulty of the task given the different topics of the passages in our dataset and conclude with recommendations and suggestions that might be of value to people working on future DADC tasks and related annotation interfaces.
abstract_zh: 我们展示了作为注释者为第一届动态对抗数据收集 (DADC) 研讨会任务 1 的提取 QA 创建高质量的对抗性机器阅读理解数据的经验。 DADC 是一种新兴的数据收集范例，模型和人类都参与其中。我们建立了一个准实验性注释设计，并对具有不同数量注释者的组进行定量分析，重点关注成功的对抗性攻击、成本分析和注释者置信度相关性。考虑到数据集中段落的不同主题，我们进一步对我们感知到的任务难度进行定性分析，并得出建议和建议，这些建议和建议可能对从事未来 DADC 任务和相关注释界面的人员有价值。

AI.tldr: 本文主要任务是通过结合人类和模型的循环，收集高质量的对抗数据以用于机器阅读理解任务。
AI.motivation: 为了解决动态对抗数据收集中的挑战，提高对抗数据的质量和效率，从而推动机器阅读理解模型的鲁棒性研究。
AI.method: 采用准实验标注设计，通过不同数量的标注者进行定量分析，包括对抗攻击成功率、成本分析和标注者置信度相关性，并结合不同主题的定性分析。
AI.result: 结果表明，增加标注者数量能提高对抗攻击成功率，但成本上升；同时，标注者置信度与数据质量相关，不同主题的难度影响任务效率。
AI.conclusion: 本文建议在未来的对抗数据收集任务中优化标注者配置，结合主题难度调整策略，并改进标注界面以提高效率和数据质量。

authors_text: Keiji Shinzato,Naoki Yoshinaga,Yandi Xia,Wei-Te Chen
title: Simple and Effective Knowledge-Driven Query Expansion for QA-Based Product Attribute Extraction
title_zh: 用于基于 QA 的产品属性提取的简单有效的知识驱动查询扩展
abstract: A key challenge in attribute value extraction (AVE) from e-commerce sites is how to handle a large number of attributes for diverse products. Although this challenge is partially addressed by a question answering (QA) approach which finds a value in product data for a given query (attribute), it does not work effectively for rare and ambiguous queries. We thus propose simple knowledge-driven query expansion based on possible answers (values) of a query (attribute) for QA-based AVE. We retrieve values of a query (attribute) from the training data to expand the query. We train a model with two tricks, knowledge dropout and knowledge token mixing, which mimic the imperfection of the value knowledge in testing. Experimental results on our cleaned version of AliExpress dataset show that our method improves the performance of AVE (+6.08 macro F1), especially for rare and ambiguous attributes (+7.82 and +6.86 macro F1, respectively).
summary: A key challenge in attribute value extraction (AVE) from e-commerce sites is how to handle a large number of attributes for diverse products. Although this challenge is partially addressed by a question answering (QA) approach which finds a value in product data for a given query (attribute), it does not work effectively for rare and ambiguous queries. We thus propose simple knowledge-driven query expansion based on possible answers (values) of a query (attribute) for QA-based AVE. We retrieve values of a query (attribute) from the training data to expand the query. We train a model with two tricks, knowledge dropout and knowledge token mixing, which mimic the imperfection of the value knowledge in testing. Experimental results on our cleaned version of AliExpress dataset show that our method improves the performance of AVE (+6.08 macro F1), especially for rare and ambiguous attributes (+7.82 and +6.86 macro F1, respectively).
abstract_zh: 从电子商务网站进行属性值提取（AVE）的一个关键挑战是如何处理不同产品的大量属性。尽管问答 (QA) 方法可以部分解决这一挑战，该方法可以在产品数据中查找给定查询（属性）的值，但它对于罕见且不明确的查询并不能有效地工作。因此，我们根据基于 QA 的 AVE 的查询（属性）的可能答案（值）提出简单的知识驱动查询扩展。我们从训练数据中检索查询（属性）的值以扩展查询。我们用两种技巧训练模型：知识丢失和知识令牌混合，模仿测试中价值知识的不完美性。我们清理后的 AliExpress 数据集版本的实验结果表明，我们的方法提高了 AVE 的性能（+6.08 宏 F1），特别是对于罕见和模糊的属性（分别为 +7.82 和 +6.86 宏 F1）。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于知识驱动的查询扩展方法，用于改进基于问答（QA）的产品属性值提取（AVE）任务。
AI.motivation: 现有QA方法在处理电商产品属性提取时，对罕见和模糊属性的查询效果不佳，需提升模型鲁棒性。
AI.method: 通过从训练数据中检索查询的可能值扩展原始查询，并采用知识dropout和知识token mixing两种训练技巧模拟测试时的知识不完善性。
AI.result: 在AliExpress数据集上，宏观F1指标提升6.08，其中罕见属性提升7.82，模糊属性提升6.86。
AI.conclusion: 知识驱动的查询扩展方法有效提升了AVE性能，尤其在处理稀有和模糊属性时表现显著。

authors_text: Zanyar Zohourianshahzadi,Terrance E. Boult,Jugal K. Kalita
title: ZoDIAC: Zoneout Dropout Injection Attention Calculation
title_zh: ZoDIAC：Zoneout Dropout注入注意力计算
comment: This work was published at IEEE AIxSET 2024 (DOI: 10.1109/AIxSET62544.2024.00008). Arxiv didn't allow a new submission for the journal version (DOI: 10.1142/S1793351X25440039), so both versions with separate DOIs are merged into one Arxiv entry to reflect the latest journal updates
comments: This work was published at IEEE AIxSET 2024 (DOI: 10.1109/AIxSET62544.2024.00008). Arxiv didn't allow a new submission for the journal version (DOI: 10.1142/S1793351X25440039), so both versions with separate DOIs are merged into one Arxiv entry to reflect the latest journal updates
abstract: In the past few years the transformer model has been utilized for a variety of tasks such as image captioning, image classification natural language generation, and natural language understanding. As a key component of the transformer model, self-attention calculates the attention values by mapping the relationships among the head elements of the source and target sequence, yet there is no explicit mechanism to refine and intensify the attention values with respect to the context of the input and target sequences. Based on this intuition, we introduce a novel refine and intensify attention mechanism that is called Zoneup Dropout Injection Attention Calculation (ZoDIAC), in which the intensities of attention values in the elements of the input source and target sequences are first refined using GELU and dropout and then intensified using a proposed zoneup process which includes the injection of a learned scalar factor. Our extensive experiments show that ZoDIAC achieves statistically significant higher scores under all image captioning metrics using various feature extractors in comparison to the conventional self-attention module in the transformer model on the MS-COCO dataset. Our proposed ZoDIAC attention modules can be used as a drop-in replacement for the attention components in all transformer models. The code for our experiments is publicly available at: https://github.com/zanyarz/zodiac
summary: In the past few years the transformer model has been utilized for a variety of tasks such as image captioning, image classification natural language generation, and natural language understanding. As a key component of the transformer model, self-attention calculates the attention values by mapping the relationships among the head elements of the source and target sequence, yet there is no explicit mechanism to refine and intensify the attention values with respect to the context of the input and target sequences. Based on this intuition, we introduce a novel refine and intensify attention mechanism that is called Zoneup Dropout Injection Attention Calculation (ZoDIAC), in which the intensities of attention values in the elements of the input source and target sequences are first refined using GELU and dropout and then intensified using a proposed zoneup process which includes the injection of a learned scalar factor. Our extensive experiments show that ZoDIAC achieves statistically significant higher scores under all image captioning metrics using various feature extractors in comparison to the conventional self-attention module in the transformer model on the MS-COCO dataset. Our proposed ZoDIAC attention modules can be used as a drop-in replacement for the attention components in all transformer models. The code for our experiments is publicly available at: https://github.com/zanyarz/zodiac
abstract_zh: 在过去的几年中，Transformer 模型已被用于各种任务，例如图像字幕、图像分类自然语言生成和自然语言理解。作为 Transformer 模型的关键组成部分，自注意力通过映射源序列和目标序列的头元素之间的关系来计算注意力值，但没有明确的机制来细化和强化相对于输入序列和目标序列的上下文的注意力值。基于这种直觉，我们引入了一种新颖的细化和强化注意力机制，称为 Zoneup Dropout Injection Attention Calculation (ZoDIAC)，其中输入源和目标序列的元素中的注意力值的强度首先使用 GELU 和 dropout 进行细化，然后使用建议的 zoneup 过程进行强化，其中包括注入学习的标量因子。我们的大量实验表明，与 MS-COCO 数据集上的 Transformer 模型中的传统自注意力模块相比，ZoDIAC 在使用各种特征提取器的所有图像字幕指标下取得了统计上显着的更高分数。我们提出的 ZoDIAC 注意力模块可以用作所有变压器模型中注意力组件的直接替代品。我们实验的代码可公开获取：https://github.com/zanyarz/zodiac

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为ZoDIAC的新型注意力机制，用于改进Transformer模型中的自注意力计算，以提升图像描述等任务的性能。
AI.motivation: 传统Transformer的自注意力机制缺乏对输入和目标序列上下文的显式优化，导致注意力值无法有效细化和增强。
AI.method: 通过GELU激活函数和Dropout对注意力值进行细化，再结合引入可学习标量因子的Zoneup过程对注意力值进行强化。
AI.result: 在MS-COCO数据集上，ZoDIAC在多种特征提取器下均显著优于传统自注意力模块，图像描述指标表现更优。
AI.conclusion: ZoDIAC可作为Transformer模型中注意力组件的即插即用替代方案，有效提升模型性能。

search_text: ZoDIAC: Zoneout Dropout Injection Attention Calculation ZoDIAC：Zoneout Dropout注入注意力计算 In the past few years the transformer model has been utilized for a variety of tasks such as image captioning, image classification natural language generation, and natural language understanding. As a key component of the transformer model, self-attention calculates the attention values by mapping the relationships among the head elements of the source and target sequence, yet there is no explicit mechanism to refine and intensify the attention values with respect to the context of the input and target sequences. Based on this intuition, we introduce a novel refine and intensify attention mechanism that is called Zoneup Dropout Injection Attention Calculation (ZoDIAC), in which the intensities of attention values in the elements of the input source and target sequences are first refined using GELU and dropout and then intensified using a proposed zoneup process which includes the injection of a learned scalar factor. Our extensive experiments show that ZoDIAC achieves statistically significant higher scores under all image captioning metrics using various feature extractors in comparison to the conventional self-attention module in the transformer model on the MS-COCO dataset. Our proposed ZoDIAC attention modules can be used as a drop-in replacement for the attention components in all transformer models. The code for our experiments is publicly available at: https://github.com/zanyarz/zodiac 在过去的几年中，Transformer 模型已被用于各种任务，例如图像字幕、图像分类自然语言生成和自然语言理解。作为 Transformer 模型的关键组成部分，自注意力通过映射源序列和目标序列的头元素之间的关系来计算注意力值，但没有明确的机制来细化和强化相对于输入序列和目标序列的上下文的注意力值。基于这种直觉，我们引入了一种新颖的细化和强化注意力机制，称为 Zoneup Dropout Injection Attention Calculation (ZoDIAC)，其中输入源和目标序列的元素中的注意力值的强度首先使用 GELU 和 dropout 进行细化，然后使用建议的 zoneup 过程进行强化，其中包括注入学习的标量因子。我们的大量实验表明，与 MS-COCO 数据集上的 Transformer 模型中的传统自注意力模块相比，ZoDIAC 在使用各种特征提取器的所有图像字幕指标下取得了统计上显着的更高分数。我们提出的 ZoDIAC 注意力模块可以用作所有变压器模型中注意力组件的直接替代品。我们实验的代码可公开获取：https://github.com/zanyarz/zodiac 本文提出了一种名为ZoDIAC的新型注意力机制，用于改进Transformer模型中的自注意力计算，以提升图像描述等任务的性能。 Zanyar Zohourianshahzadi,Terrance E. Boult,Jugal K. Kalita cs.CV

authors_text: Sumeet Menon,David Chapman
title: Semi-supervised Contrastive Outlier removal for Pseudo Expectation Maximization (SCOPE)
title_zh: 伪期望最大化的半监督对比离群值去除（SCOPE）
abstract: Semi-supervised learning is the problem of training an accurate predictive model by combining a small labeled dataset with a presumably much larger unlabeled dataset. Many methods for semi-supervised deep learning have been developed, including pseudolabeling, consistency regularization, and contrastive learning techniques. Pseudolabeling methods however are highly susceptible to confounding, in which erroneous pseudolabels are assumed to be true labels in early iterations, thereby causing the model to reinforce its prior biases and thereby fail to generalize to strong predictive performance. We present a new approach to suppress confounding errors through a method we describe as Semi-supervised Contrastive Outlier removal for Pseudo Expectation Maximization (SCOPE). Like basic pseudolabeling, SCOPE is related to Expectation Maximization (EM), a latent variable framework which can be extended toward understanding cluster-assumption deep semi-supervised algorithms. However, unlike basic pseudolabeling which fails to adequately take into account the probability of the unlabeled samples given the model, SCOPE introduces an outlier suppression term designed to improve the behavior of EM iteration given a discrimination DNN backbone in the presence of outliers. Our results show that SCOPE greatly improves semi-supervised classification accuracy over a baseline, and furthermore when combined with consistency regularization achieves the highest reported accuracy for the semi-supervised CIFAR-10 classification task using 250 and 4000 labeled samples. Moreover, we show that SCOPE reduces the prevalence of confounding errors during pseudolabeling iterations by pruning erroneous high-confidence pseudolabeled samples that would otherwise contaminate the labeled set in subsequent retraining iterations.
summary: Semi-supervised learning is the problem of training an accurate predictive model by combining a small labeled dataset with a presumably much larger unlabeled dataset. Many methods for semi-supervised deep learning have been developed, including pseudolabeling, consistency regularization, and contrastive learning techniques. Pseudolabeling methods however are highly susceptible to confounding, in which erroneous pseudolabels are assumed to be true labels in early iterations, thereby causing the model to reinforce its prior biases and thereby fail to generalize to strong predictive performance. We present a new approach to suppress confounding errors through a method we describe as Semi-supervised Contrastive Outlier removal for Pseudo Expectation Maximization (SCOPE). Like basic pseudolabeling, SCOPE is related to Expectation Maximization (EM), a latent variable framework which can be extended toward understanding cluster-assumption deep semi-supervised algorithms. However, unlike basic pseudolabeling which fails to adequately take into account the probability of the unlabeled samples given the model, SCOPE introduces an outlier suppression term designed to improve the behavior of EM iteration given a discrimination DNN backbone in the presence of outliers. Our results show that SCOPE greatly improves semi-supervised classification accuracy over a baseline, and furthermore when combined with consistency regularization achieves the highest reported accuracy for the semi-supervised CIFAR-10 classification task using 250 and 4000 labeled samples. Moreover, we show that SCOPE reduces the prevalence of confounding errors during pseudolabeling iterations by pruning erroneous high-confidence pseudolabeled samples that would otherwise contaminate the labeled set in subsequent retraining iterations.
abstract_zh: 半监督学习是通过将小型标记数据集与可能更大的未标记数据集相结合来训练准确的预测模型的问题。已经开发了许多半监督深度学习方法，包括伪标签、一致性正则化和对比学习技术。然而，伪标签方法非常容易受到混杂的影响，其中错误的伪标签在早期迭代中被假定为真实标签，从而导致模型强化其先验偏差，从而无法泛化为强大的预测性能。我们提出了一种抑制混杂错误的新方法，通过我们描述为伪期望最大化的半监督对比离群值去除（SCOPE）的方法。与基本的伪标签一样，SCOPE 与期望最大化（EM）相关，这是一种潜在变量框架，可以扩展到理解集群假设深度半监督算法。然而，与无法充分考虑给定模型的未标记样本的概率的基本伪标记不同，SCOPE 引入了异常值抑制项，旨在在存在异常值的情况下在给定判别 DNN 主干的情况下改善 EM 迭代的行为。我们的结果表明，SCOPE 在基线上极大地提高了半监督分类精度，此外，与一致性正则化结合使用时，使用 250 个和 4000 个标记样本的半监督 CIFAR-10 分类任务实现了报告的最高精度。此外，我们还表明，SCOPE 通过修剪错误的高置信度伪标记样本来减少伪标记迭代期间混杂错误的发生率，否则这些样本会在后续的再训练迭代中污染标记集。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SCOPE方法，通过半监督对比异常值去除技术改进伪期望最大化算法，以提升半监督分类任务的准确性。
AI.motivation: 伪标签方法在半监督学习中易受混淆误差影响，错误伪标签会被视为真实标签，导致模型强化先验偏见并影响泛化性能。
AI.method: SCOPE基于EM框架，引入异常值抑制项，通过判别DNN主干网络在迭代中剪枝高置信度但错误的伪标签样本，优化EM迭代行为。
AI.result: SCOPE在CIFAR-10数据集上使用250和4000个标注样本时，结合一致性正则化达到半监督分类最高准确率，并显著减少混淆误差。
AI.conclusion: SCOPE通过有效抑制伪标签中的异常值，提升了半监督学习的鲁棒性和分类准确性，为处理小样本标注场景提供了新思路。

authors_text: Doğay Kamar,Nazım Kemal Üre,Gözde Ünal
title: GAN-based Intrinsic Exploration For Sample Efficient Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 基于GAN的样本高效强化学习内在探索
abstract: In this study, we address the problem of efficient exploration in reinforcement learning. Most common exploration approaches depend on random action selection, however these approaches do not work well in environments with sparse or no rewards. We propose Generative Adversarial Network-based Intrinsic Reward Module that learns the distribution of the observed states and sends an intrinsic reward that is computed as high for states that are out of distribution, in order to lead agent to unexplored states. We evaluate our approach in Super Mario Bros for a no reward setting and in Montezuma's Revenge for a sparse reward setting and show that our approach is indeed capable of exploring efficiently. We discuss a few weaknesses and conclude by discussing future works.
summary: In this study, we address the problem of efficient exploration in reinforcement learning. Most common exploration approaches depend on random action selection, however these approaches do not work well in environments with sparse or no rewards. We propose Generative Adversarial Network-based Intrinsic Reward Module that learns the distribution of the observed states and sends an intrinsic reward that is computed as high for states that are out of distribution, in order to lead agent to unexplored states. We evaluate our approach in Super Mario Bros for a no reward setting and in Montezuma's Revenge for a sparse reward setting and show that our approach is indeed capable of exploring efficiently. We discuss a few weaknesses and conclude by discussing future works.
abstract_zh: 在这项研究中，我们解决了强化学习中的有效探索问题。最常见的探索方法依赖于随机动作选择，但是这些方法在奖励稀疏或没有奖励的环境中效果不佳。我们提出了基于生成对抗网络的内在奖励模块，该模块学习观察到的状态的分布，并发送一个内在奖励，该奖励对于分布之外的状态计算得较高，以便引导智能体进入未探索的状态。我们在《超级马里奥兄弟》中的无奖励设置和《蒙特祖玛的复仇》中的稀疏奖励设置中评估了我们的方法，并表明我们的方法确实能够有效地进行探索。我们讨论了一些弱点，并通过讨论未来的工作来结束。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于生成对抗网络（GAN）的内在奖励模块，用于解决强化学习中的高效探索问题，特别是在稀疏或无奖励环境下。
AI.motivation: 传统探索方法依赖随机动作选择，在稀疏或无奖励环境中难以有效引导智能体探索，需设计更高效的内在激励机制。
AI.method: 构建GAN模型学习已观测状态分布，通过计算分布外状态的高内在奖励，驱动智能体主动探索未访问状态。
AI.result: 在《超级马里奥兄弟》无奖励场景和《蒙特祖玛的复仇》稀疏奖励场景中验证，证明方法能显著提升探索效率。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在特定环境下表现优异，但存在泛化性限制，未来需优化模型鲁棒性并探索更复杂的奖励机制。

search_text: GAN-based Intrinsic Exploration For Sample Efficient Reinforcement Learning 基于GAN的样本高效强化学习内在探索 In this study, we address the problem of efficient exploration in reinforcement learning. Most common exploration approaches depend on random action selection, however these approaches do not work well in environments with sparse or no rewards. We propose Generative Adversarial Network-based Intrinsic Reward Module that learns the distribution of the observed states and sends an intrinsic reward that is computed as high for states that are out of distribution, in order to lead agent to unexplored states. We evaluate our approach in Super Mario Bros for a no reward setting and in Montezuma's Revenge for a sparse reward setting and show that our approach is indeed capable of exploring efficiently. We discuss a few weaknesses and conclude by discussing future works. 在这项研究中，我们解决了强化学习中的有效探索问题。最常见的探索方法依赖于随机动作选择，但是这些方法在奖励稀疏或没有奖励的环境中效果不佳。我们提出了基于生成对抗网络的内在奖励模块，该模块学习观察到的状态的分布，并发送一个内在奖励，该奖励对于分布之外的状态计算得较高，以便引导智能体进入未探索的状态。我们在《超级马里奥兄弟》中的无奖励设置和《蒙特祖玛的复仇》中的稀疏奖励设置中评估了我们的方法，并表明我们的方法确实能够有效地进行探索。我们讨论了一些弱点，并通过讨论未来的工作来结束。 本文提出一种基于生成对抗网络（GAN）的内在奖励模块，用于解决强化学习中的高效探索问题，特别是在稀疏或无奖励环境下。 Doğay Kamar,Nazım Kemal Üre,Gözde Ünal cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CV

authors_text: Alexander Black,Tu Bui,Simon Jenni,Zhifei Zhang,Viswanathan Swaminanthan,John Collomosse
title: SImProv: Scalable Image Provenance Framework for Robust Content Attribution
title_zh: SImProv：用于强大内容归因的可扩展图像来源框架
comment: Under consideration at Computer Vision and Image Understanding
comments: Under consideration at Computer Vision and Image Understanding
abstract: We present SImProv - a scalable image provenance framework to match a query image back to a trusted database of originals and identify possible manipulations on the query. SImProv consists of three stages: a scalable search stage for retrieving top-k most similar images; a re-ranking and near-duplicated detection stage for identifying the original among the candidates; and finally a manipulation detection and visualization stage for localizing regions within the query that may have been manipulated to differ from the original. SImProv is robust to benign image transformations that commonly occur during online redistribution, such as artifacts due to noise and recompression degradation, as well as out-of-place transformations due to image padding, warping, and changes in size and shape. Robustness towards out-of-place transformations is achieved via the end-to-end training of a differentiable warping module within the comparator architecture. We demonstrate effective retrieval and manipulation detection over a dataset of 100 million images.
summary: We present SImProv - a scalable image provenance framework to match a query image back to a trusted database of originals and identify possible manipulations on the query. SImProv consists of three stages: a scalable search stage for retrieving top-k most similar images; a re-ranking and near-duplicated detection stage for identifying the original among the candidates; and finally a manipulation detection and visualization stage for localizing regions within the query that may have been manipulated to differ from the original. SImProv is robust to benign image transformations that commonly occur during online redistribution, such as artifacts due to noise and recompression degradation, as well as out-of-place transformations due to image padding, warping, and changes in size and shape. Robustness towards out-of-place transformations is achieved via the end-to-end training of a differentiable warping module within the comparator architecture. We demonstrate effective retrieval and manipulation detection over a dataset of 100 million images.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 SImProv - 一个可扩展的图像来源框架，用于将查询图像与可信的原始数据库进行匹配，并识别对查询的可能操作。 SImProv 由三个阶段组成：一个可扩展的搜索阶段，用于检索前 k 个最相似的图像；重新排序和近似重复检测阶段，用于识别候选者中的原始者；最后是操纵检测和可视化阶段，用于定位查询中可能已被操纵以不同于原始区域的区域。 SImProv 对于在线重新分配期间常见的良性图像变换具有鲁棒性，例如由于噪声和再压缩退化而导致的伪影，以及由于图像填充、扭曲以及大小和形状变化而导致的错位变换。通过比较器架构内可微翘曲模块的端到端训练来实现对异地变换的鲁棒性。我们展示了对 1 亿张图像的数据集的有效检索和操纵检测。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SImProv框架，用于通过可信数据库追溯查询图像来源并检测可能的篡改。
AI.motivation: 随着网络上图像的广泛传播和篡改，需要一种鲁棒的图像溯源方法以识别原始来源并检测篡改行为。
AI.method: SImProv包含三个阶段：1) 可扩展的相似图像检索；2) 重排序与近似重复检测；3) 篡改定位与可视化。通过端到端训练的可微分变形模块增强对几何变换的鲁棒性。
AI.result: 在1000万张图像数据集上验证了框架的有效性，能够处理噪声、重压缩、填充、变形等常见图像变换。
AI.conclusion: SImProv为大规模图像溯源提供了高效且鲁棒的解决方案，可有效应对在线传播中的图像篡改问题。

authors_text: Younggyo Seo,Danijar Hafner,Hao Liu,Fangchen Liu,Stephen James,Kimin Lee,Pieter Abbeel
title: Masked World Models for Visual Control
title_zh: 用于视觉控制的蒙面世界模型
comment: Project website: https://sites.google.com/view/mwm-rl. Accepted to CoRL 2022
comments: Project website: https://sites.google.com/view/mwm-rl. Accepted to CoRL 2022
abstract: Visual model-based reinforcement learning (RL) has the potential to enable sample-efficient robot learning from visual observations. Yet the current approaches typically train a single model end-to-end for learning both visual representations and dynamics, making it difficult to accurately model the interaction between robots and small objects. In this work, we introduce a visual model-based RL framework that decouples visual representation learning and dynamics learning. Specifically, we train an autoencoder with convolutional layers and vision transformers (ViT) to reconstruct pixels given masked convolutional features, and learn a latent dynamics model that operates on the representations from the autoencoder. Moreover, to encode task-relevant information, we introduce an auxiliary reward prediction objective for the autoencoder. We continually update both autoencoder and dynamics model using online samples collected from environment interaction. We demonstrate that our decoupling approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on a variety of visual robotic tasks from Meta-world and RLBench, e.g., we achieve 81.7% success rate on 50 visual robotic manipulation tasks from Meta-world, while the baseline achieves 67.9%. Code is available on the project website: https://sites.google.com/view/mwm-rl.
summary: Visual model-based reinforcement learning (RL) has the potential to enable sample-efficient robot learning from visual observations. Yet the current approaches typically train a single model end-to-end for learning both visual representations and dynamics, making it difficult to accurately model the interaction between robots and small objects. In this work, we introduce a visual model-based RL framework that decouples visual representation learning and dynamics learning. Specifically, we train an autoencoder with convolutional layers and vision transformers (ViT) to reconstruct pixels given masked convolutional features, and learn a latent dynamics model that operates on the representations from the autoencoder. Moreover, to encode task-relevant information, we introduce an auxiliary reward prediction objective for the autoencoder. We continually update both autoencoder and dynamics model using online samples collected from environment interaction. We demonstrate that our decoupling approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on a variety of visual robotic tasks from Meta-world and RLBench, e.g., we achieve 81.7% success rate on 50 visual robotic manipulation tasks from Meta-world, while the baseline achieves 67.9%. Code is available on the project website: https://sites.google.com/view/mwm-rl.
abstract_zh: 基于视觉模型的强化学习 (RL) 有潜力使机器人能够通过视觉观察进行样本高效的学习。然而，当前的方法通常会端到端地训练单个模型来学习视觉表示和动力学，因此很难准确地建模机器人和小物体之间的交互。在这项工作中，我们引入了一种基于视觉模型的 RL 框架，该框架将视觉表示学习和动态学习解耦。具体来说，我们训练具有卷积层和视觉变换器（ViT）的自动编码器，以在给定掩码卷积特征的情况下重建像素，并学习对自动编码器的表示进行操作的潜在动力学模型。此外，为了编码任务相关信息，我们为自动编码器引入了辅助奖励预测目标。我们使用从环境交互中收集的在线样本不断更新自动编码器和动力学模型。我们证明，我们的解耦方法在 Meta-world 和 RLBench 的各种视觉机器人任务上实现了最先进的性能，例如，我们在 Meta-world 的 50 个视觉机器人操作任务上实现了 81.7% 的成功率，而基线达到了 67.9%。代码可在项目网站上找到：https://sites.google.com/view/mwm-rl。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种解耦的视觉模型基于强化学习框架，通过分离视觉表示学习和动力学学习，提升视觉机器人任务的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有方法将视觉表示与动力学学习耦合，难以准确建模机器人与小物体的交互，导致样本效率低下。
AI.method: 使用结合卷积层和ViT的自编码器重建掩码特征，引入辅助奖励预测目标，并通过在线样本持续更新自编码器与动力学模型。
AI.result: 在Meta-world和RLBench的50项视觉机器人任务中，成功率达81.7%，显著优于基线方法的67.9%。
AI.conclusion: 解耦视觉表示与动力学学习能有效提升视觉控制任务性能，验证了该框架的优越性。

We continually update both autoencoder and dynamics model using online samples collected from environment interaction. We demonstrate that our decoupling approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on a variety of visual robotic tasks from Meta-world and RLBench, e.g., we achieve 81.7% success rate on 50 visual robotic manipulation tasks from Meta-world, while the baseline achieves 67.9%. Code is available on the project website: https://sites.google.com/view/mwm-rl. 基于视觉模型的强化学习 (RL) 有潜力使机器人能够通过视觉观察进行样本高效的学习。然而，当前的方法通常会端到端地训练单个模型来学习视觉表示和动力学，因此很难准确地建模机器人和小物体之间的交互。在这项工作中，我们引入了一种基于视觉模型的 RL 框架，该框架将视觉表示学习和动态学习解耦。具体来说，我们训练具有卷积层和视觉变换器（ViT）的自动编码器，以在给定掩码卷积特征的情况下重建像素，并学习对自动编码器的表示进行操作的潜在动力学模型。此外，为了编码任务相关信息，我们为自动编码器引入了辅助奖励预测目标。我们使用从环境交互中收集的在线样本不断更新自动编码器和动力学模型。我们证明，我们的解耦方法在 Meta-world 和 RLBench 的各种视觉机器人任务上实现了最先进的性能，例如，我们在 Meta-world 的 50 个视觉机器人操作任务上实现了 81.7% 的成功率，而基线达到了 67.9%。代码可在项目网站上找到：https://sites.google.com/view/mwm-rl。 本文提出一种解耦的视觉模型基于强化学习框架，通过分离视觉表示学习和动力学学习，提升视觉机器人任务的性能。 Younggyo Seo,Danijar Hafner,Hao Liu,Fangchen Liu,Stephen James,Kimin Lee,Pieter Abbeel cs.RO,cs.AI,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Nicolas PD Sawaya,Joonsuk Huh
title: Improved resource-tunable near-term quantum algorithms for transition probabilities, with applications in physics and variational quantum linear algebra
title_zh: 改进了用于转移概率的资源可调近期量子算法，在物理学和变分量子线性代数中的应用
abstract: Transition amplitudes and transition probabilities are relevant to many areas of physics simulation, including the calculation of response properties and correlation functions. These quantities can also be related to solving linear systems of equations. Here we present three related algorithms for calculating transition probabilities. First, we extend a previously published short-depth algorithm, allowing for the two input states to be non-orthogonal. Building on this first procedure, we then derive a higher-depth algorithm based on Trotterization and Richardson extrapolation that requires fewer circuit evaluations. Third, we introduce a tunable algorithm that allows for trading off circuit depth and measurement complexity, yielding an algorithm that can be tailored to specific hardware characteristics. Finally, we implement proof-of-principle numerics for models in physics and chemistry and for a subroutine in variational quantum linear solving (VQLS). The primary benefits of our approaches are that (a) arbitrary non-orthogonal states may now be used with small increases in quantum resources, (b) we (like another recently proposed method) entirely avoid subroutines such as the Hadamard test that may require three-qubit gates to be decomposed, and (c) in some cases fewer quantum circuit evaluations are required as compared to the previous state-of-the-art in NISQ algorithms for transition probabilities.
summary: Transition amplitudes and transition probabilities are relevant to many areas of physics simulation, including the calculation of response properties and correlation functions. These quantities can also be related to solving linear systems of equations. Here we present three related algorithms for calculating transition probabilities. First, we extend a previously published short-depth algorithm, allowing for the two input states to be non-orthogonal. Building on this first procedure, we then derive a higher-depth algorithm based on Trotterization and Richardson extrapolation that requires fewer circuit evaluations. Third, we introduce a tunable algorithm that allows for trading off circuit depth and measurement complexity, yielding an algorithm that can be tailored to specific hardware characteristics. Finally, we implement proof-of-principle numerics for models in physics and chemistry and for a subroutine in variational quantum linear solving (VQLS). The primary benefits of our approaches are that (a) arbitrary non-orthogonal states may now be used with small increases in quantum resources, (b) we (like another recently proposed method) entirely avoid subroutines such as the Hadamard test that may require three-qubit gates to be decomposed, and (c) in some cases fewer quantum circuit evaluations are required as compared to the previous state-of-the-art in NISQ algorithms for transition probabilities.
abstract_zh: 转变幅度和转变概率与物理模拟的许多领域相关，包括响应特性和相关函数的计算。这些量也可能与求解线性方程组有关。这里我们提出了三种用于计算转移概率的相关算法。首先，我们扩展了之前发布的短深度算法，允许两个输入状态是非正交的。在此第一个过程的基础上，我们基于 Trotterization 和 Richardson 外推推导了一种更深入的算法，该算法需要更少的电路评估。第三，我们引入了一种可调算法，可以权衡电路深度和测量复杂性，从而产生一种可以根据特定硬件特性进行定制的算法。最后，我们为物理和化学模型以及变分量子线性求解 (VQLS) 中的子程序实现了原理验证数值。我们的方法的主要好处是（a）现在可以使用任意非正交状态，同时量子资源略有增加，（b）我们（像最近提出的另一种方法一样）完全避免了可能需要分解三量子位门的哈达玛测试等子程序，以及（c）在某些情况下，与之前最先进的 NISQ 转移概率算法相比，需要更少的量子电路评估。

AI.tldr: 本文提出三种改进的近期量子算法，用于计算转移概率，适用于物理模拟和变分量子线性代数领域。
AI.motivation: 转移概率在物理模拟和线性方程组求解中至关重要，但现有方法存在资源消耗大或依赖复杂量子门分解的缺陷，需更高效的算法。
AI.method: 扩展短深度算法以支持非正交态输入；基于Trotter化和Richardson外推法设计高深度算法减少电路评估；提出可调节算法平衡电路深度与测量复杂度。
AI.result: 数值验证显示算法在物理化学模型及VQLS子程序中有效，且具备非正交态兼容性、无需分解三量子门、减少电路评估次数等优势。
AI.conclusion: 新算法在资源效率和硬件适应性上优于现有方法，为NISQ设备的物理模拟和量子线性代数问题提供更优解决方案。

authors_text: Anurag Sarkar,Seth Cooper
title: Latent Combinational Game Design
title_zh: 潜在组合游戏设计
comment: 10 pages, 9 figures, IEEE Transactions on Games
comments: 10 pages, 9 figures, IEEE Transactions on Games
abstract: We present latent combinational game design -- an approach for generating playable games that blend a given set of games in a desired combination using deep generative latent variable models. We use Gaussian Mixture Variational Autoencoders (GMVAEs) which model the VAE latent space via a mixture of Gaussian components. Through supervised training, each component encodes levels from one game and lets us define blended games as linear combinations of these components. This enables generating new games that blend the input games as well as controlling the relative proportions of each game in the blend. We also extend prior blending work using conditional VAEs and compare against the GMVAE and additionally introduce a hybrid conditional GMVAE (CGMVAE) architecture which lets us generate whole blended levels and layouts. Results show that these approaches can generate playable games that blend the input games in specified combinations. We use both platformers and dungeon-based games to demonstrate our results.
summary: We present latent combinational game design -- an approach for generating playable games that blend a given set of games in a desired combination using deep generative latent variable models. We use Gaussian Mixture Variational Autoencoders (GMVAEs) which model the VAE latent space via a mixture of Gaussian components. Through supervised training, each component encodes levels from one game and lets us define blended games as linear combinations of these components. This enables generating new games that blend the input games as well as controlling the relative proportions of each game in the blend. We also extend prior blending work using conditional VAEs and compare against the GMVAE and additionally introduce a hybrid conditional GMVAE (CGMVAE) architecture which lets us generate whole blended levels and layouts. Results show that these approaches can generate playable games that blend the input games in specified combinations. We use both platformers and dungeon-based games to demonstrate our results.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了潜在的组合游戏设计——一种生成可玩游戏的方法，该方法使用深度生成潜在变量模型将一组给定的游戏混合成所需的组合。我们使用高斯混合变分自编码器 (GMVAE)，它通过混合高斯分量对 VAE 潜在空间进行建模。通过监督训练，每个组件对一个游戏的级别进行编码，并让我们将混合游戏定义为这些组件的线性组合。这使得能够生成混合输入游戏的新游戏以及控制混合中每个游戏的相对比例。我们还使用条件 VAE 扩展了先前的混合工作，并与 GMVAE 进行比较，此外还引入了混合条件 GMVAE (CGMVAE) 架构，该架构使我们能够生成完整的混合关卡和布局。结果表明，这些方法可以生成可玩的游戏，将输入游戏混合成指定的组合。我们使用平台游戏和基于地下城的游戏来展示我们的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度生成模型的潜在组合游戏设计方法，通过混合高斯变分自编码器（GMVAE）实现游戏关卡的组合生成。
AI.motivation: 传统游戏设计难以灵活融合不同游戏元素，需开发可控制游戏组合比例的生成方法以增强创作灵活性。
AI.method: 采用监督训练的GMVAE建模游戏关卡潜在空间，通过高斯组件线性组合实现游戏混合，并扩展为条件GMVAE（CGMVAE）生成完整关卡布局。
AI.result: 实验表明方法可生成符合指定组合比例的可玩游戏，成功应用于平台跳跃类和地牢类游戏的混合生成。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为游戏设计提供了可控的组合生成框架，验证了潜在空间线性混合在游戏创作中的有效性。

authors_text: Yuqing Wang,Yun Zhao,Linda Petzold
title: Predicting the Need for Blood Transfusion in Intensive Care Units with Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 通过强化学习预测重症监护病房的输血需求
comment: Proceedings of 13th International ACM Conference on Bioinformatics, Computational Biology and Health Informatics
comments: Proceedings of 13th International ACM Conference on Bioinformatics, Computational Biology and Health Informatics
abstract: As critically ill patients frequently develop anemia or coagulopathy, transfusion of blood products is a frequent intervention in the Intensive Care Units (ICU). However, inappropriate transfusion decisions made by physicians are often associated with increased risk of complications and higher hospital costs. In this work, we aim to develop a decision support tool that uses available patient information for transfusion decision-making on three common blood products (red blood cells, platelets, and fresh frozen plasma). To this end, we adopt an off-policy batch reinforcement learning (RL) algorithm, namely, discretized Batch Constrained Q-learning, to determine the best action (transfusion or not) given observed patient trajectories. Simultaneously, we consider different state representation approaches and reward design mechanisms to evaluate their impacts on policy learning. Experiments are conducted on two real-world critical care datasets: the MIMIC-III and the UCSF. Results demonstrate that policy recommendations on transfusion achieved comparable matching against true hospital policies via accuracy and weighted importance sampling evaluations on the MIMIC-III dataset. Furthermore, a combination of transfer learning (TL) and RL on the data-scarce UCSF dataset can provide up to $17.02% improvement in terms of accuracy, and up to 18.94% and 21.63% improvement in jump-start and asymptotic performance in terms of weighted importance sampling averaged over three transfusion tasks. Finally, simulations on transfusion decisions suggest that the transferred RL policy could reduce patients' estimated 28-day mortality rate by 2.74% and decreased acuity rate by 1.18% on the UCSF dataset.
summary: As critically ill patients frequently develop anemia or coagulopathy, transfusion of blood products is a frequent intervention in the Intensive Care Units (ICU). However, inappropriate transfusion decisions made by physicians are often associated with increased risk of complications and higher hospital costs. In this work, we aim to develop a decision support tool that uses available patient information for transfusion decision-making on three common blood products (red blood cells, platelets, and fresh frozen plasma). To this end, we adopt an off-policy batch reinforcement learning (RL) algorithm, namely, discretized Batch Constrained Q-learning, to determine the best action (transfusion or not) given observed patient trajectories. Simultaneously, we consider different state representation approaches and reward design mechanisms to evaluate their impacts on policy learning. Experiments are conducted on two real-world critical care datasets: the MIMIC-III and the UCSF. Results demonstrate that policy recommendations on transfusion achieved comparable matching against true hospital policies via accuracy and weighted importance sampling evaluations on the MIMIC-III dataset. Furthermore, a combination of transfer learning (TL) and RL on the data-scarce UCSF dataset can provide up to $17.02% improvement in terms of accuracy, and up to 18.94% and 21.63% improvement in jump-start and asymptotic performance in terms of weighted importance sampling averaged over three transfusion tasks. Finally, simulations on transfusion decisions suggest that the transferred RL policy could reduce patients' estimated 28-day mortality rate by 2.74% and decreased acuity rate by 1.18% on the UCSF dataset.
abstract_zh: 由于危重患者经常出现贫血或凝血障碍，输血制品是重症监护病房（ICU）的常见干预措施。然而，医生做出的不适当的输血决定通常会增加并发症的风险和更高的住院费用。在这项工作中，我们的目标是开发一种决策支持工具，利用现有的患者信息对三种常见血液制品（红细胞、血小板和新鲜冰冻血浆）进行输血决策。为此，我们采用离策略批量强化学习（RL）算法，即离散批量约束 Q 学习，来确定给定观察到的患者轨迹的最佳动作（是否输血）。同时，我们考虑不同的状态表示方法和奖励设计机制来评估它们对政策学习的影响。在两个现实世界的重症监护数据集：MIMIC-III 和 UCSF 上进行了实验。结果表明，通过 MIMIC-III 数据集的准确性和加权重要性抽样评估，输血政策建议与真实的医院政策实现了可比较的匹配。此外，在数据稀缺的 UCSF 数据集上结合迁移学习 (TL) 和 RL 可以在准确性方面提供高达 17.02% 的改进，并且在三个输血任务的加权重要性采样方面的快速启动和渐近性能提高高达 18.94% 和 21.63%。最后，输血决策的模拟表明，在 UCSF 数据集上，转移的 RL 策略可以将患者的估计 28 天死亡率降低 2.74%，并将急性率降低 1.18%。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在开发一种基于强化学习的决策支持工具，用于重症监护病房中三种常见血液制品（红细胞、血小板和新鲜冰冻血浆）的输血决策。
AI.motivation: 重症患者常出现贫血或凝血障碍，但医生的输血决策常导致并发症增加和医疗成本上升，亟需优化决策支持系统以提高输血合理性。
AI.method: 采用离散化批量约束Q学习（off-policy batch RL）算法，结合不同状态表示方法和奖励机制设计，并在MIMIC-III和UCSF两个真实重症数据集上验证，同时引入迁移学习提升数据稀缺场景下的性能。
AI.result: 在MIMIC-III数据集上，策略推荐准确率与医院真实政策匹配度高；在UCSF数据集上，迁移学习结合RL使准确率提升17.02%，加权重要性采样性能提升18.94%-21.63%；模拟显示可降低28天死亡率2.74%和病情恶化率1.18%。
AI.conclusion: 基于强化学习的输血决策模型能有效辅助临床实践，尤其在数据稀缺场景下结合迁移学习可显著提升决策准确性与患者预后效果。

search_text: Predicting the Need for Blood Transfusion in Intensive Care Units with Reinforcement Learning 通过强化学习预测重症监护病房的输血需求 As critically ill patients frequently develop anemia or coagulopathy, transfusion of blood products is a frequent intervention in the Intensive Care Units (ICU). However, inappropriate transfusion decisions made by physicians are often associated with increased risk of complications and higher hospital costs. In this work, we aim to develop a decision support tool that uses available patient information for transfusion decision-making on three common blood products (red blood cells, platelets, and fresh frozen plasma). To this end, we adopt an off-policy batch reinforcement learning (RL) algorithm, namely, discretized Batch Constrained Q-learning, to determine the best action (transfusion or not) given observed patient trajectories. Simultaneously, we consider different state representation approaches and reward design mechanisms to evaluate their impacts on policy learning. Experiments are conducted on two real-world critical care datasets: the MIMIC-III and the UCSF. Results demonstrate that policy recommendations on transfusion achieved comparable matching against true hospital policies via accuracy and weighted importance sampling evaluations on the MIMIC-III dataset. Furthermore, a combination of transfer learning (TL) and RL on the data-scarce UCSF dataset can provide up to $17.02% improvement in terms of accuracy, and up to 18.94% and 21.63% improvement in jump-start and asymptotic performance in terms of weighted importance sampling averaged over three transfusion tasks. Finally, simulations on transfusion decisions suggest that the transferred RL policy could reduce patients' estimated 28-day mortality rate by 2.74% and decreased acuity rate by 1.18% on the UCSF dataset. 由于危重患者经常出现贫血或凝血障碍，输血制品是重症监护病房（ICU）的常见干预措施。然而，医生做出的不适当的输血决定通常会增加并发症的风险和更高的住院费用。在这项工作中，我们的目标是开发一种决策支持工具，利用现有的患者信息对三种常见血液制品（红细胞、血小板和新鲜冰冻血浆）进行输血决策。为此，我们采用离策略批量强化学习（RL）算法，即离散批量约束 Q 学习，来确定给定观察到的患者轨迹的最佳动作（是否输血）。同时，我们考虑不同的状态表示方法和奖励设计机制来评估它们对政策学习的影响。在两个现实世界的重症监护数据集：MIMIC-III 和 UCSF 上进行了实验。结果表明，通过 MIMIC-III 数据集的准确性和加权重要性抽样评估，输血政策建议与真实的医院政策实现了可比较的匹配。此外，在数据稀缺的 UCSF 数据集上结合迁移学习 (TL) 和 RL 可以在准确性方面提供高达 17.02% 的改进，并且在三个输血任务的加权重要性采样方面的快速启动和渐近性能提高高达 18.94% 和 21.63%。最后，输血决策的模拟表明，在 UCSF 数据集上，转移的 RL 策略可以将患者的估计 28 天死亡率降低 2.74%，并将急性率降低 1.18%。 本文旨在开发一种基于强化学习的决策支持工具，用于重症监护病房中三种常见血液制品（红细胞、血小板和新鲜冰冻血浆）的输血决策。 Yuqing Wang,Yun Zhao,Linda Petzold cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Saeed Saadatnejad,Yi Zhou Ju,Alexandre Alahi
title: Pedestrian 3D Bounding Box Prediction
title_zh: 行人 3D 边界框预测
comment: Accepted and published in hEART2022 (the 10th Symposium of the European Association for Research in Transportation): http://www.heart-web.org/
comments: Accepted and published in hEART2022 (the 10th Symposium of the European Association for Research in Transportation): http://www.heart-web.org/
abstract: Safety is still the main issue of autonomous driving, and in order to be globally deployed, they need to predict pedestrians' motions sufficiently in advance. While there is a lot of research on coarse-grained (human center prediction) and fine-grained predictions (human body keypoints prediction), we focus on 3D bounding boxes, which are reasonable estimates of humans without modeling complex motion details for autonomous vehicles. This gives the flexibility to predict in longer horizons in real-world settings. We suggest this new problem and present a simple yet effective model for pedestrians' 3D bounding box prediction. This method follows an encoder-decoder architecture based on recurrent neural networks, and our experiments show its effectiveness in both the synthetic (JTA) and real-world (NuScenes) datasets. The learned representation has useful information to enhance the performance of other tasks, such as action anticipation. Our code is available online: https://github.com/vita-epfl/bounding-box-prediction
summary: Safety is still the main issue of autonomous driving, and in order to be globally deployed, they need to predict pedestrians' motions sufficiently in advance. While there is a lot of research on coarse-grained (human center prediction) and fine-grained predictions (human body keypoints prediction), we focus on 3D bounding boxes, which are reasonable estimates of humans without modeling complex motion details for autonomous vehicles. This gives the flexibility to predict in longer horizons in real-world settings. We suggest this new problem and present a simple yet effective model for pedestrians' 3D bounding box prediction. This method follows an encoder-decoder architecture based on recurrent neural networks, and our experiments show its effectiveness in both the synthetic (JTA) and real-world (NuScenes) datasets. The learned representation has useful information to enhance the performance of other tasks, such as action anticipation. Our code is available online: https://github.com/vita-epfl/bounding-box-prediction
abstract_zh: 安全仍然是自动驾驶的主要问题，为了在全球范围内部署，它们需要提前充分预测行人的运动。虽然有很多关于粗粒度（人体中心预测）和细粒度预测（人体关键点预测）的研究，但我们专注于 3D 边界框，这是对人体的合理估计，无需为自动驾驶车辆的复杂运动细节建模。这提供了在现实世界环境中进行更长期预测的灵活性。我们提出了这个新问题，并提出了一个简单而有效的行人 3D 边界框预测模型。该方法遵循基于循环神经网络的编码器-解码器架构，我们的实验证明了其在合成（JTA）和现实世界（NuScenes）数据集中的有效性。学习到的表示具有有用的信息，可以提高其他任务的性能，例如动作预期。我们的代码可在线获取：https://github.com/vita-epfl/bounding-box-prediction

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种用于自动驾驶场景中行人3D包围盒预测的新方法，以提升安全性和运动预测能力。
AI.motivation: 自动驾驶安全需提前预测行人运动，现有研究多关注人体中心点或关键点预测，而3D包围盒能更合理地估计行人形态且适用于长时域预测。
AI.method: 基于编码器-解码器架构的循环神经网络模型，通过序列学习预测行人3D包围盒。
AI.result: 在合成数据集JTA和真实数据集NuScenes上验证了方法有效性，且所学表征可增强动作预测等下游任务性能。
AI.conclusion: 提出了行人3D包围盒预测新问题，设计了简单有效的模型，实验表明其在合成与真实场景均具实用性。

authors_text: Victor Vikram Odouard,Melanie Mitchell
title: Evaluating Understanding on Conceptual Abstraction Benchmarks
title_zh: 评估对概念抽象基准的理解
comment: EBeM'22: AI Evaluation Beyond Metrics, July 24, 2022, Vienna, Austria
comments: EBeM'22: AI Evaluation Beyond Metrics, July 24, 2022, Vienna, Austria
abstract: A long-held objective in AI is to build systems that understand concepts in a humanlike way. Setting aside the difficulty of building such a system, even trying to evaluate one is a challenge, due to present-day AI's relative opacity and its proclivity for finding shortcut solutions. This is exacerbated by humans' tendency to anthropomorphize, assuming that a system that can recognize one instance of a concept must also understand other instances, as a human would. In this paper, we argue that understanding a concept requires the ability to use it in varied contexts. Accordingly, we propose systematic evaluations centered around concepts, by probing a system's ability to use a given concept in many different instantiations. We present case studies of such an evaluations on two domains -- RAVEN (inspired by Raven's Progressive Matrices) and the Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC) -- that have been used to develop and assess abstraction abilities in AI systems. Our concept-based approach to evaluation reveals information about AI systems that conventional test sets would have left hidden.
summary: A long-held objective in AI is to build systems that understand concepts in a humanlike way. Setting aside the difficulty of building such a system, even trying to evaluate one is a challenge, due to present-day AI's relative opacity and its proclivity for finding shortcut solutions. This is exacerbated by humans' tendency to anthropomorphize, assuming that a system that can recognize one instance of a concept must also understand other instances, as a human would. In this paper, we argue that understanding a concept requires the ability to use it in varied contexts. Accordingly, we propose systematic evaluations centered around concepts, by probing a system's ability to use a given concept in many different instantiations. We present case studies of such an evaluations on two domains -- RAVEN (inspired by Raven's Progressive Matrices) and the Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC) -- that have been used to develop and assess abstraction abilities in AI systems. Our concept-based approach to evaluation reveals information about AI systems that conventional test sets would have left hidden.
abstract_zh: 人工智能的一个长期目标是构建以类人方式理解概念的系统。抛开构建这样一个系统的难度不谈，即使是尝试评估一个系统也是一项挑战，因为当今人工智能的相对不透明性及其寻找捷径解决方案的倾向。人类拟人化的倾向加剧了这种情况，假设一个能够识别概念的一个实例的系统也必须像人类一样理解其他实例。在本文中，我们认为理解一个概念需要能够在不同的上下文中使用它。因此，我们提出以概念为中心的系统评估，通过探索系统在许多不同实例中使用给定概念的能力。我们提出了对两个领域进行此类评估的案例研究——RAVEN（受 Raven 的渐进矩阵启发）和抽象与推理语料库 (ARC)——这两个领域已用于开发和评估人工智能系统中的抽象能力。我们基于概念的评估方法揭示了传统测试集隐藏的有关人工智能系统的信息。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于概念的系统性评估方法，通过在不同情境下测试AI系统对概念的应用能力，以更准确地评估其理解水平。
AI.motivation: 现有AI评估方法难以揭示系统对概念的真正理解能力，因AI可能依赖捷径解决方案且人类易过度拟人化其能力。
AI.method: 设计概念驱动的评估框架，在RAVEN和ARC等基准测试中，通过多样化实例检验系统对概念的迁移应用能力。
AI.result: 该方法揭示了传统测试集未能发现的AI系统局限性，证明当前AI在跨情境概念应用上存在显著不足。
AI.conclusion: 基于概念的评估能更深入暴露AI理解能力的缺陷，为开发真正具备人类水平概念理解的AI提供关键评价手段。

search_text: Evaluating Understanding on Conceptual Abstraction Benchmarks 评估对概念抽象基准的理解 A long-held objective in AI is to build systems that understand concepts in a humanlike way. Setting aside the difficulty of building such a system, even trying to evaluate one is a challenge, due to present-day AI's relative opacity and its proclivity for finding shortcut solutions. This is exacerbated by humans' tendency to anthropomorphize, assuming that a system that can recognize one instance of a concept must also understand other instances, as a human would. In this paper, we argue that understanding a concept requires the ability to use it in varied contexts. Accordingly, we propose systematic evaluations centered around concepts, by probing a system's ability to use a given concept in many different instantiations. We present case studies of such an evaluations on two domains -- RAVEN (inspired by Raven's Progressive Matrices) and the Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC) -- that have been used to develop and assess abstraction abilities in AI systems. Our concept-based approach to evaluation reveals information about AI systems that conventional test sets would have left hidden. 人工智能的一个长期目标是构建以类人方式理解概念的系统。抛开构建这样一个系统的难度不谈，即使是尝试评估一个系统也是一项挑战，因为当今人工智能的相对不透明性及其寻找捷径解决方案的倾向。人类拟人化的倾向加剧了这种情况，假设一个能够识别概念的一个实例的系统也必须像人类一样理解其他实例。在本文中，我们认为理解一个概念需要能够在不同的上下文中使用它。因此，我们提出以概念为中心的系统评估，通过探索系统在许多不同实例中使用给定概念的能力。我们提出了对两个领域进行此类评估的案例研究——RAVEN（受 Raven 的渐进矩阵启发）和抽象与推理语料库 (ARC)——这两个领域已用于开发和评估人工智能系统中的抽象能力。我们基于概念的评估方法揭示了传统测试集隐藏的有关人工智能系统的信息。 本文提出了一种基于概念的系统性评估方法，通过在不同情境下测试AI系统对概念的应用能力，以更准确地评估其理解水平。 Victor Vikram Odouard,Melanie Mitchell cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Yao Yan,Thomas Yu,Kathleen Muenzen,Sijia Liu,Connor Boyle,George Koslowski,Jiaxin Zheng,Nicholas Dobbins,Clement Essien,Hongfang Liu,Larsson Omberg,Meliha Yestigen,Bradley Taylor,James A Eddy,Justin Guinney,Sean Mooney,Thomas Schaffter
title: The NLP Sandbox: an efficient model-to-data system to enable federated and unbiased evaluation of clinical NLP models
title_zh: NLP 沙箱：一种高效的模型到数据系统，可对临床 NLP 模型进行联合且公正的评估
abstract: Objective The evaluation of natural language processing (NLP) models for clinical text de-identification relies on the availability of clinical notes, which is often restricted due to privacy concerns. The NLP Sandbox is an approach for alleviating the lack of data and evaluation frameworks for NLP models by adopting a federated, model-to-data approach. This enables unbiased federated model evaluation without the need for sharing sensitive data from multiple institutions. Materials and Methods We leveraged the Synapse collaborative framework, containerization software, and OpenAPI generator to build the NLP Sandbox (nlpsandbox.io). We evaluated two state-of-the-art NLP de-identification focused annotation models, Philter and NeuroNER, using data from three institutions. We further validated model performance using data from an external validation site. Results We demonstrated the usefulness of the NLP Sandbox through de-identification clinical model evaluation. The external developer was able to incorporate their model into the NLP Sandbox template and provide user experience feedback. Discussion We demonstrated the feasibility of using the NLP Sandbox to conduct a multi-site evaluation of clinical text de-identification models without the sharing of data. Standardized model and data schemas enable smooth model transfer and implementation. To generalize the NLP Sandbox, work is required on the part of data owners and model developers to develop suitable and standardized schemas and to adapt their data or model to fit the schemas. Conclusions The NLP Sandbox lowers the barrier to utilizing clinical data for NLP model evaluation and facilitates federated, multi-site, unbiased evaluation of NLP models.
summary: Objective The evaluation of natural language processing (NLP) models for clinical text de-identification relies on the availability of clinical notes, which is often restricted due to privacy concerns. The NLP Sandbox is an approach for alleviating the lack of data and evaluation frameworks for NLP models by adopting a federated, model-to-data approach. This enables unbiased federated model evaluation without the need for sharing sensitive data from multiple institutions. Materials and Methods We leveraged the Synapse collaborative framework, containerization software, and OpenAPI generator to build the NLP Sandbox (nlpsandbox.io). We evaluated two state-of-the-art NLP de-identification focused annotation models, Philter and NeuroNER, using data from three institutions. We further validated model performance using data from an external validation site. Results We demonstrated the usefulness of the NLP Sandbox through de-identification clinical model evaluation. The external developer was able to incorporate their model into the NLP Sandbox template and provide user experience feedback. Discussion We demonstrated the feasibility of using the NLP Sandbox to conduct a multi-site evaluation of clinical text de-identification models without the sharing of data. Standardized model and data schemas enable smooth model transfer and implementation. To generalize the NLP Sandbox, work is required on the part of data owners and model developers to develop suitable and standardized schemas and to adapt their data or model to fit the schemas. Conclusions The NLP Sandbox lowers the barrier to utilizing clinical data for NLP model evaluation and facilitates federated, multi-site, unbiased evaluation of NLP models.
abstract_zh: 目的 用于临床文本去识别的自然语言处理（NLP）模型的评估依赖于临床笔记的可用性，而临床笔记往往由于隐私问题而受到限制。 NLP 沙箱是一种通过采用联邦模型到数据的方法来缓解 NLP 模型数据和评估框架缺乏的方法。这可以实现公正的联合模型评估，而无需共享多个机构的敏感数据。材料和方法我们利用 Synapse 协作框架、容器化软件和 OpenAPI 生成器来构建 NLP 沙箱 (nlpsandbox.io)。我们使用来自三个机构的数据评估了两种最先进的 NLP 去识别注释模型 Philter 和 NeuroNER。我们使用来自外部验证站点的数据进一步验证了模型性能。结果我们通过去识别化临床模型评估证明了NLP沙箱的实用性。外部开发人员能够将他们的模型合并到 NLP 沙盒模板中并提供用户体验反馈。讨论我们证明了使用 NLP 沙盒在不共享数据的情况下对临床文本去识别模型进行多站点评估的可行性。标准化的模型和数据模式可实现平稳的模型传输和实施。为了推广 NLP 沙盒，数据所有者和模型开发人员需要努力开发合适的标准化模式，并调整其数据或模型以适应模式。结论 NLP沙箱降低了利用临床数据进行NLP模型评估的门槛，有利于NLP模型的联合、多站点、公正评估。

AI.tldr: 本文提出NLP Sandbox系统，通过联邦和无偏方式解决临床NLP模型评估中数据隐私限制的问题。
AI.motivation: 临床NLP模型评估受限于隐私导致的临床数据获取困难，需在不共享敏感数据的前提下实现多机构联合评估。
AI.method: 基于Synapse框架、容器化技术及OpenAPI生成器构建系统，采用模型到数据的联邦架构，使用三家机构数据验证Philter和NeuroNER模型，并通过外部站点验证。
AI.result: 验证了系统有效性，外部开发者可集成模型并反馈体验，证明多站点无数据共享评估的可行性。
AI.conclusion: NLP Sandbox降低了临床数据评估门槛，推动联邦式无偏评估，但需数据方和开发者协作制定标准化接口。

authors_text: Sangyun Lee,Gyojung Gu,Sunghyun Park,Seunghwan Choi,Jaegul Choo
title: High-Resolution Virtual Try-On with Misalignment and Occlusion-Handled Conditions
title_zh: 具有错位和遮挡处理条件的高分辨率虚拟试戴
abstract: Image-based virtual try-on aims to synthesize an image of a person wearing a given clothing item. To solve the task, the existing methods warp the clothing item to fit the person's body and generate the segmentation map of the person wearing the item before fusing the item with the person. However, when the warping and the segmentation generation stages operate individually without information exchange, the misalignment between the warped clothes and the segmentation map occurs, which leads to the artifacts in the final image. The information disconnection also causes excessive warping near the clothing regions occluded by the body parts, so-called pixel-squeezing artifacts. To settle the issues, we propose a novel try-on condition generator as a unified module of the two stages (i.e., warping and segmentation generation stages). A newly proposed feature fusion block in the condition generator implements the information exchange, and the condition generator does not create any misalignment or pixel-squeezing artifacts. We also introduce discriminator rejection that filters out the incorrect segmentation map predictions and assures the performance of virtual try-on frameworks. Experiments on a high-resolution dataset demonstrate that our model successfully handles the misalignment and occlusion, and significantly outperforms the baselines. Code is available at https://github.com/sangyun884/HR-VITON.
summary: Image-based virtual try-on aims to synthesize an image of a person wearing a given clothing item. To solve the task, the existing methods warp the clothing item to fit the person's body and generate the segmentation map of the person wearing the item before fusing the item with the person. However, when the warping and the segmentation generation stages operate individually without information exchange, the misalignment between the warped clothes and the segmentation map occurs, which leads to the artifacts in the final image. The information disconnection also causes excessive warping near the clothing regions occluded by the body parts, so-called pixel-squeezing artifacts. To settle the issues, we propose a novel try-on condition generator as a unified module of the two stages (i.e., warping and segmentation generation stages). A newly proposed feature fusion block in the condition generator implements the information exchange, and the condition generator does not create any misalignment or pixel-squeezing artifacts. We also introduce discriminator rejection that filters out the incorrect segmentation map predictions and assures the performance of virtual try-on frameworks. Experiments on a high-resolution dataset demonstrate that our model successfully handles the misalignment and occlusion, and significantly outperforms the baselines. Code is available at https://github.com/sangyun884/HR-VITON.
abstract_zh: 基于图像的虚拟试穿旨在合成穿着给定服装的人的图像。为了解决该任务，现有方法将服装物品扭曲以适合人的身体，并在将物品与人融合之前生成穿着该物品的人的分割图。然而，当变形和分割生成阶段在没有信息交换的情况下单独运行时，变形的衣服和分割图之间会发生未对准，从而导致最终图像中出现伪影。信息断开还会导致身体部位遮挡的衣服区域附近出现过度扭曲，即所谓的像素挤压伪影。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一种新颖的试穿条件生成器作为两个阶段（即变形和分割生成阶段）的统一模块。条件生成器中新提出的特征融合块实现了信息交换，并且条件生成器不会产生任何未对准或像素挤压伪影。我们还引入了鉴别器拒绝，可以过滤掉不正确的分割图预测并确保虚拟试穿框架的性能。在高分辨率数据集上的实验表明，我们的模型成功地处理了错位和遮挡，并且显着优于基线。代码可在 https://github.com/sangyun884/HR-VITON 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种高分辨率虚拟试穿方法，解决衣物变形与人体分割图之间的错位及遮挡问题。
AI.motivation: 现有虚拟试穿方法因衣物变形与分割图生成阶段信息隔离，导致错位和像素挤压伪影，影响最终图像质量。
AI.method: 设计统一的条件生成器整合变形与分割阶段，通过特征融合模块实现信息交互，并引入判别器拒绝机制过滤错误分割图。
AI.result: 在高分辨率数据集上验证，模型有效处理错位与遮挡问题，显著优于基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过统一模块和特征交互解决虚拟试穿中的关键问题，实现高质量高分辨率试穿效果。
